visual_notes: The banded 'Inca Rose' stalactitic form from Argentina — polished to reveal concentric pink-and-white rings — is one of the most recognized ornamental stone patterns in the gem world. Crystallized raspberry-red specimens from Colorado are among the most sought-after collector mineral specimens globally.
prompt_fragments: raspberry-red curved rhombohedral crystals on white quartz matrix
Inca Rose banded stalactite — concentric pink and cream rings
warm rose-manganese architecture of Sweet Home Mine
vibe_notes: Rhodochrosite lives between pink and red — the color of living tissue, roses, and sunsets. The Inca Rose banding reads as geological diary — each layer a year of cave drip. The Sweet Home crystals are raspberry-red architectural perfection.
folklore_summary: Argentine tradition holds that rhodochrosite (Inca Rose) was formed from the blood of ancient Inca kings and queens crystallized over time — giving the stone its name 'Rosa del Inca' (Rose of the Inca).
